JobCircular.title: JobCircular
JobCircular.description: Represents a single job circular or advertisement published by the admin.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobCircular entity.

title.description: The official title of the job position being advertised.

description.description: Full details of the job circular, including responsibilities, requirements, and benefits.

salaryInformation.description: Information about the salary or pay scale for the position.

applicationDeadline.description: The date and time by which applicants must submit their applications.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the job circular is currently active and accepting applications.

publishedDate.description: The date and time when the job circular was first published.

updatedDate.description: The date and time when the job circular was last updated.

Applicant.title: Applicant
Applicant.description: Stores the personal and contact information of an individual applicant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Applicant entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the applicant.

lastName.description: The last name of the applicant.

fathersName.description: The father's name of the applicant.

mothersName.description: The mother's name of the applicant.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the applicant.

gender.description: The gender of the applicant (e.g., Male, Female, Other).

nidNumber.description: The National ID (NID) number of the applicant.

email.description: The email address of the applicant.

mobileNumber.description: The mobile number of the applicant.

presentStreet.description: The street address for the applicant's present address.

presentCity.description: The city for the applicant's present address.

presentDistrict.description: The district for the applicant's present address.

presentPostalCode.description: The postal code for the applicant's present address.

permanentStreet.description: The street address for the applicant's permanent address.

permanentCity.description: The city for the applicant's permanent address.

permanentDistrict.description: The district for the applicant's permanent address.

permanentPostalCode.description: The postal code for the applicant's permanent address.

maritalStatus.description: The marital status of the applicant (e.g., Single, Married).

EducationalQualification.title: EducationalQualification
EducationalQualification.description: Details of an applicant's educational background.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EducationalQualification entity.

applicantId.description: Reference to the Applicant who holds this qualification. (Relationship: Applicant 1:N EducationalQualification)

degreeName.description: The name of the degree or examination (e.g., SSC, HSC, Bachelor of Science).

institutionName.description: The name of the educational institution attended.

passingYear.description: The year the qualification was obtained.

result.description: The result obtained (e.g., GPA, CGPA, Division, Grade).

boardUniversity.description: The name of the board or university that awarded the qualification.

Application.title: Application
Application.description: Represents a single application submitted by an applicant for a specific job circular.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Application entity, serving as the auto-generated User ID for payment and tracking.

applicantId.description: Reference to the Applicant who submitted this application. (Relationship: Applicant 1:N Application)

jobCircularId.description: Reference to the JobCircular this application is for. (Relationship: JobCircular 1:N Application)

applicationDate.description: The date and time when the application was submitted.

paymentStatus.description: The current payment status of the application (e.g., Unpaid, Pending, Paid, Cancelled).

paymentId.description: Reference to the Payment entity if a payment has been made for this application. (Relationship: Application 1:1 Payment)

photoUrl.description: URL or path to the applicant's uploaded photo.

signatureUrl.description: URL or path to the applicant's uploaded signature.

invoiceUrl.description: URL or path to the auto-generated PDF invoice/applicant copy.

admitCardId.description: Reference to the AdmitCard entity generated for this application. (Relationship: Application 1:1 AdmitCard)

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records details of a payment transaction made by an applicant for an application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity, often the transaction ID from the payment gateway.

applicationId.description: Reference to the Application associated with this payment. (Relationship: Application 1:1 Payment)

gatewayTransactionId.description: The actual transaction ID provided by the payment gateway (e.g., bKash, Nagad, Rocket).

currency.description: The currency of the payment (e.g., BDT).

paymentGateway.description: The payment gateway used for the transaction (e.g., bKash, Nagad, Rocket).

paymentDate.description: The date and time when the payment was successfully processed.

status.description: The status of the payment transaction (e.g., Pending, Success, Failed, Refunded).

verifiedByAdmin.description: Indicates if this payment has been manually verified by an administrator.

AdmitCard.title: AdmitCard
AdmitCard.description: Contains details necessary for an applicant's admit card, including exam information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdmitCard entity.

applicationId.description: Reference to the Application for which this admit card is generated. (Relationship: Application 1:1 AdmitCard)

examLocation.description: The physical location or venue where the examination will take place.

instructions.description: Any special instructions or guidelines for the examinee.

downloadUrl.description: URL or path to the generated PDF admit card for download.

generatedDate.description: The date and time when the admit card was generated.

AdminRole.title: AdminRole
AdminRole.description: Defines an administrator role. The document ID is the user's UID.

role.description: The role of the user, e.g., 'admin'.

assignedAt.description: The timestamp when the role was assigned.

definition.description: Stores details of active and inactive job circulars. Publicly readable by all users. Write access is restricted to authenticated administrators. No get() required for authorization due to global read access.

params.name: jobCircularId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific job circular.

definition.description: Stores the primary profile information for an applicant. Access is strictly controlled such that only the applicant themselves (matching 'request.auth.uid' with '{applicantId}') can read/write their own profile, or an administrator can read. This structure uses path-based ownership for authorization independence.

params.name: applicantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the applicant, matching Firebase 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: Stores educational qualifications associated with a specific applicant. Access is limited to the owning applicant (based on 'applicantId' in the path, which matches 'request.auth.uid') and administrators. The 'applicantId' in the path provides authorization independence.

params.name: applicantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the owning applicant, matching Firebase 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: educationalQualificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific educational qualification record.

definition.description: Stores a specific job application submitted by an applicant for a job circular. Access is restricted to the owning applicant (matching 'request.auth.uid' with '{applicantId}') and administrators. The 'applicantId' in the path enables authorization independence.

params.name: applicantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the owning applicant, matching Firebase 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific application instance.

definition.description: Records details of a payment transaction for a specific application. Access is restricted to the owning applicant and administrators. The 'applicantId' in the path ensures authorization independence.

params.name: applicantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the owning applicant, matching Firebase 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the associated application.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific payment transaction.

definition.description: Contains details for an applicant's admit card, linked to a specific application. Read access is for the owning applicant and administrators. Write access is for administrators only. The 'applicantId' in the path provides authorization independence.

params.name: applicantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the owning applicant, matching Firebase 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the associated application.

params.name: admitCardId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific admit card.

definition.description: This collection contains documents where the document ID ('adminUid') corresponds to the 'uid' of a Firebase authenticated user who has administrator privileges. The *existence* of a document here grants admin rights. Used for DBAC (Database Access Control) and enables authorization independence for admin actions.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The unique identifier for an administrator, matching Firebase 'request.auth.uid'.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering strictly to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and QAPs (Query-Aware Rules).

1.  **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
    *   **Path-Based Ownership Denormalization:** For all applicant-related data (Applicant profiles, Educational Qualifications, Applications, Payments, Admit Cards), authorization context is denormalized directly into the document path. For instance, paths like `/applicants/{applicantId}/applications/{applicationId}` embed the owner's unique ID (`applicantId`) directly. This allows security rules to evaluate `request.auth.uid == applicantId` without needing any `get()` calls to check parent documents for ownership, making operations atomic and highly performant.
    *   **Admin Role Denormalization:** Administrative privileges are handled via a dedicated top-level collection, `/roles_admin/{adminUid}`. The mere existence of a document with the `adminUid` matching `request.auth.uid` grants admin rights. This ensures that admin checks (`exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`) are independent of other data collections and do not rely on hierarchical lookups.

2.  **QAPs (Query-Aware Rules):**
    *   **Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security:**
        *   The `/jobCirculars` collection has a homogeneous security posture: public read for all users, admin write. This allows applicants to list all job circulars efficiently (`allow list, read: if true;`), and rules for write access only check if the user is an admin.
        *   Applicant-specific subcollections (e.g., `/applicants/{applicantId}/applications`) also maintain homogeneous security: owner-specific read/write, admin read. When an applicant lists their own applications, the rule `allow list, read: if request.auth.uid == applicantId;` is applied. This rule doesn't filter the query based on document content, but rather restricts the *scope* of the query to the user's own path, fully supporting QAPs for owner-specific lists.
    *   **Admin Listing:** Administrators can perform broad `list` queries across all applicant data (e.g., all applications for all applicants) because their `isAdmin()` rule grants them read access to entire collections, without forcing client-side filtering or inefficient security rule evaluations.

3.  **Clarity of Intent & Debuggability:** The path structure clearly delineates ownership and data relationships. For example, it's immediately apparent that `/applicants/{applicantId}/applications/{applicationId}` represents an application belonging to `applicantId`. Security rules become straightforward `if request.auth.uid == applicantId` or `if isAdmin()`, making them easy to write, understand, and debug.
